# Beudox HR — Technical Context for AI Assistants

## Project Overview
Beudox HR is a comprehensive, multi-tenant workforce management platform built with React, TypeScript, and Supabase. It provides complete HR lifecycle management including employee onboarding, payroll, evaluations, leave management, and financial reporting.

## Architecture & Technology Stack

### Frontend Architecture
- **Framework**: React 18 with TypeScript
- **Build Tool**: Vite with SWC for fast compilation
- **Routing**: React Router v6 with protected routes
- **State Management**: React Query for server state, Context API for authentication
- **UI Framework**: shadcn/ui built on Radix UI primitives
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS with custom design tokens
- **Forms**: React Hook Form with Zod schema validation
- **Charts**: Recharts for data visualization
- **Rich Text**: Tiptap editor for HR policy documents

### Backend Architecture
- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Supabase
- **Authentication**: Supabase Auth with Row Level Security (RLS)
- **API**: Supabase client with real-time subscriptions
- **Storage**: Supabase Storage for file uploads
- **Edge Functions**: Supabase Edge Functions for server-side logic
- **Email**: Supabase email service for notifications

### Key Design Patterns
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of compound components and render props
- **Custom Hooks**: Business logic abstracted into reusable hooks
- **Provider Pattern**: Context providers for auth, theming, and global state
- **Compound Components**: Complex UI components built from simpler primitives
- **Render Props**: Flexible component APIs for customization

## Database Schema & Relationships

### Core Entities
- **companies**: Multi-tenant isolation
- **employees**: User profiles with auth integration
- **roles**: Permission-based access control
- **departments**: Organizational structure

### HR Operations
- **evaluations**: Bi-annual performance reviews
- **daily_evaluations**: Quick feedback system
- **leave_requests**: Time-off management
- **payroll_records**: Salary processing
- **loans**: Employee loan tracking

### Business Operations
- **projects**: Project management
- **clients**: Client relationship management
- **invoices**: Billing and invoicing
- **monthly_expenses**: Financial tracking

### Configuration
- **hr_policies**: Policy document management
- **public_holidays**: Holiday calendar
- **leave_types**: Configurable leave categories
- **evaluation_parameters**: Performance criteria

## Authentication & Authorization

### User Roles & Permissions
- **CEO**: Full system access, company-wide visibility
- **HR Manager**: Employee management, payroll, settings
- **Team Lead**: Team oversight, evaluations, project management
- **Employee**: Personal profile, leave requests, payslips

### Security Implementation
- **Row Level Security**: Database-level access control
- **JWT Tokens**: Session management via Supabase Auth
- **Route Guards**: Frontend route protection
- **API Policies**: Backend function authorization

## Key Business Logic

### Payroll Processing
- Monthly payroll generation with overtime calculations
- Support for regular OT, holiday OT, bonuses, and loan deductions
- Automated payslip generation and email delivery

### Evaluation System
- Bi-annual comprehensive reviews with recommendation tracking
- Daily peer feedback with directional evaluation (received/given)
- Role-based visibility controls for sensitive information

### Leave Management
- Configurable leave types with balance tracking
- Approval workflows with notification system
- Working day calculations excluding weekends/holidays

### Financial Reporting
- Real-time dashboard with 6-month trend analysis
- Payroll vs expenses comparison
- Interactive charts with drill-down capabilities

## Development Workflow

### Code Organization
- **Components**: Modular, reusable UI components
- **Pages**: Route-level components with data fetching
- **Hooks**: Custom hooks for business logic
- **Utils**: Pure utility functions
- **Types**: TypeScript interfaces and types

### Data Fetching Strategy
- **React Query**: Caching, background refetching, optimistic updates
- **Real-time**: Supabase subscriptions for live data
- **Error Handling**: Comprehensive error boundaries and fallbacks
- **Loading States**: Skeleton components and loading indicators

### Testing Strategy
- **Unit Tests**: Vitest for component and utility testing
- **Integration Tests**: Playwright for E2E testing
- **Type Safety**: Strict TypeScript configuration

## Configuration & Environment

### Build Configuration
- **Vite**: Fast development server and optimized builds
- **TypeScript**: Strict type checking and path mapping
- **ESLint**: Code quality and consistency
- **PostCSS**: CSS processing with Tailwind

### Environment Variables
- **Supabase URL**: Backend service endpoint
- **Supabase Keys**: Public and service role keys
- **Feature Flags**: Environment-specific feature toggles

## Performance Considerations

### Frontend Optimization
- **Code Splitting**: Route-based and component lazy loading
- **Bundle Analysis**: Optimized chunk sizes
- **Image Optimization**: Responsive images and lazy loading
- **Caching**: Aggressive caching with React Query

### Database Optimization
- **Indexing**: Strategic indexes on frequently queried columns
- **RLS Policies**: Efficient row-level security implementation
- **Connection Pooling**: Supabase managed connection pooling
- **Query Optimization**: Efficient joins and aggregations

## Deployment & Infrastructure

### Build Process
- **CI/CD**: Automated testing and deployment
- **Static Assets**: Optimized asset delivery
- **Environment Config**: Environment-specific configurations

### Monitoring & Analytics
- **Error Tracking**: Client-side error monitoring
- **Performance Monitoring**: Core Web Vitals tracking
- **User Analytics**: Usage patterns and feature adoption

## Key Files & Directories

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap
- `src/App.tsx`: Main application component with routing
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Next.js style routing (though using Vite)

### Core Components
- `src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout
- `src/components/layout/AppSidebar.tsx`: Navigation sidebar
- `src/components/layout/TopBar.tsx`: Top navigation bar
- `src/components/BeudoxLogo.tsx`: Brand logo component

### Business Logic
- `src/hooks/useAuth.tsx`: Authentication context and logic
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Role-based access control
- `src/lib/notifications.ts`: Notification system
- `src/lib/leave-utils.ts`: Leave calculation utilities

### Configuration
- `vite.config.ts`: Build configuration
- `tailwind.config.ts`: Styling configuration
- `tsconfig.json`: TypeScript configuration
- `supabase/config.toml`: Backend configuration

## Common Patterns & Conventions

### Component Patterns
- **Props Interface**: Explicit TypeScript interfaces for all props
- **Default Props**: Sensible defaults with proper typing
- **Event Handlers**: Consistent naming (handleClick, onSubmit)
- **Conditional Rendering**: Early returns for loading/error states

### Data Patterns
- **Query Keys**: Consistent naming for React Query keys
- **Error Handling**: Try/catch with user-friendly error messages
- **Loading States**: Skeleton components matching content structure
- **Optimistic Updates**: Immediate UI feedback for mutations

### Styling Patterns
- **CSS Variables**: Design token system with CSS custom properties
- **Utility Classes**: Tailwind utility-first approach
- **Component Variants**: Variant props for flexible styling
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first responsive design

This technical context provides the foundation for understanding the codebase architecture, design patterns, and development practices used in Beudox HR.
